steps.title: Translate the two facts into equations
steps.content:
$x$ = length, $y$ = height.
Fact 1: Height is 64 inches → $y=64$.
Fact 2: Length is 4 times height → $x=4y$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$y = 4x$ $x = 64$’: Sets length to 64 (should be height) and says height = 4 × length (reversed). ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$y = 4x$ $y = 64$’: Correctly sets $y=64$ but reverses the relationship: says height = 4 × length instead of length = 4 × height. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$x = 4y$ $x = 64$’: Correct relationship $x=4y$, but assigns 64 to length $x$ instead of height $y$. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$x = 4y$ $y = 64$’: $x=4y$ correctly states length = 4 × height; $y=64$ correctly sets height to 64 inches. That confirms D is correct. ✓
